byWHAM

Batavia, N.Y.— A special ceremony honoring the life and legacy of a fallen local sheriff's sergeant was held Thursday.

The Special Olympics of New York hosted the Sergeant Thomas Sanfratello Memorial Law Enforcement Torch Run in Batavia.

It was held in memory of Sanfratello, a Genesee County sergeantwho died in the line of duty after he was violently attacked while escorting two people out of Batavia Downs back in March.

BACKGROUND:Pair indicted on multiple charges following Genesee County sergeant's death

Members of law enforcement agencies from across the state ran alongside athletes during Thursday's run.

"(It's great) to have everybody come together and remember him," said Investigator Chad Minuto with the Genesee County Sheriff's Office. "We've been trying to do that since his passing, and we don't ever want to forget his memory, so this is just another way to bring a tribute to a great guy and a great officer."

The torch run is an annual tradition that helps raise money and awareness for the Special Olympics of New York, which is taking place in Ithaca this weekend.

____

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Special Olympics holds fundraising torch run in memory of fallen Genesee County sergeant
